title: Six parts, two directions each — straight to hand-drawn

rule.instruction: A wall divided vertically into six equal parts, with two of the four kinds of line directions superimposed in each part.

restrictions: Faithful structure: the 300x300mm wall is divided vertically into SIX equal columns; each column is filled with TWO of the four kinds of lines (vertical, horizontal, diagonal /, diagonal \) superimposed. Monochrome black on white. RESPONDING TO ROUND-3 FEEDBACK ('boring/predictable, same feel'): line CHARACTER is the headline variable this round — the 16 executions span machine-straight -> lightly hand-drawn -> clearly not-straight -> wild (via ruledLines jitter), alongside which-pair-per-column and spacing. This injects the living/not-straight line (Klee #3/#4, and LeWitt's own 'not straight' instructions) and lets the ranking say how much life is wanted.
